commit.message:
modernize: Swift 6, @MainActor, Logger, clean platform types

GameLogic.swift:
- Add @MainActor isolation — safe to call directly from @MainActor UI code
- Replace OSLog/os_log with modern Logger API (subsystem + category)
- Remove LogLevel helper enum — Logger has native .debug/.info/.error methods
- Mark cachedWinningPatterns nonisolated(unsafe): written once per key in init,
  never mutated after; safe without a lock
- Add Sendable conformance to Player, GameState, MoveOutcome
- Simplify checkWin for-in with where clause
- Simplify getWinningPatternCoordinates using filter + map + sorted

GameScene.swift:
- Replace os_log + OSLog with Logger
- Remove NSCoder.string(for:) log arguments — use Swift string interpolation
- Remove GameEvent/GameTouchEvent type aliases (confusing indirection);
  use UITouch/UIEvent/NSEvent directly in #if os() blocks
- Use UIColor.systemRed/systemBlue/label/systemBackground/separator
  instead of hardcoded .red/.blue/.black/.white/.lightGray — respects
  dark mode and dynamic type automatically
- Add safe Array subscript extension to avoid unchecked index access
- Simplify resetGame() with array iteration over node names

TicTacToeTests.swift:
- Fix GameLogic.Player -> Player, GameLogic.GameState -> GameState
  (types are top-level, not nested inside GameLogic)
- Add @MainActor to test suite to match GameLogic actor isolation
- Mark static test argument arrays nonisolated(unsafe) to satisfy
  Swift 6 strict concurrency rules for @Test macro access

project.pbxproj:
- SWIFT_VERSION = 5.0 -> 6.0

All 16 tests pass. iOS build clean.

commit.message:
SEO: Remove 44 shadowed routes, clean config for Azure SWA

Previous deploy failed: Azure SWA rejects configs where specific routes
are shadowed by wildcard routes (e.g. /full-courses/az900fullcourse
shadowed by /full-courses/*).

Fix: Remove all 44 specific routes now covered by wildcards.
Result: 72 clean routes, 0 duplicates, 0 shadowing.

Wildcard redirect mapping:
- /hiking/* → / (old personal content)
- /books/* → / (old personal content)
- /interview-questions/* → /interview-prep/
- /study-guides/* → /cert-tracker/
- /bootcamps/* → /cloud-labs/
- /guest/* → /cloud-labs/
- /full-courses/* → /certifications/
- /ai-hub/v/* → /ai-hub/

commit.message: 🗃️ fix: ensure all preferences and filters are applied end-to-end 🗃️ - enabledEngines preference: apply saved engine list as &engines= param in streamResults() and infinite scroll URLs so the user's engine selection actually restricts which engines are queried - minDuration preference: send &min_duration= to server; server now reads the param in APISearch and passes it through handleSearchSSE to SearchStreamWithOperators, which applies max(config, user) threshold - maxHistory preference: replace hardcoded history cap of 20 with the user's maxHistory pref (0 = no limit, hard-capped at 200) - autoClearHistory preference: implemented in getHomeSearchHistory(); entries older than the configured number of days are removed and the trimmed list is persisted back to localStorage - proxyImages preference: thumbnail src now routes through /proxy/thumbnails?url= when proxyImages is true (the default) for privacy; direct URL used only when user explicitly opts out - preview_first: also sent on infinite scroll page loads (was missing) - Engine tier filter (home page): handleSearchSubmit injects a hidden input so the filter-engines dropdown value ("tier1", "tier12") is included in the form GET submission - getEnginesToUse: support "tier1" and "tier12" as special values that filter to engines whose Tier() is ≤ 1 or ≤ 2 respectively Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> src/server/handler/handlers.go src/server/service/engine/manager.go src/server/static/js/app.js

commit.message:
fix: self-bootstrapping DB schema — fix "relation character does not exist" on cold deploy (#16)

On first deploy, `altfinder` crashed immediately because `ensureSchema`
tried to `CREATE TABLE online_history` (which FKs `character`) before
`character` existed. Neither service ran `db/schema.sql` automatically,
requiring a manual psql step.

## Changes

### `AltFinderSkunkRepo.ensureSchema`
- Prepends `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` for `world`, `character`, and
`world_save_time` before `online_history` — correct dependency order,
safe to re-run

### `OnlineTrackerRepoAlg` + `OnlineTrackerSkunkRepo`
- Adds `ensureSchema: F[Unit]` to the trait
- Implements it with all 6 base tables, indexes, and the `Nefera` world
seed (`ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING`)

### `tracker/Main.scala`
- Calls `repo.ensureSchema` before starting the polling stream

### `BotApp.scala`
- Fixes Scala 3 significant-indentation warning

Whichever service starts first now creates the full schema.
`db/schema.sql` is no longer a required manual pre-deploy step.

still being crawled. These 404s damage index credibility and waste crawl budget.

Redirect categories (all 301 permanent):
- /hiking/* → / (old personal content, no equivalent)
- /books/* → / (old personal content)
- /interview-questions/* → /interview-prep/
- /study-guides/* → /cert-tracker/ (with specific mappings)
- /bootcamps/* → /cloud-labs/ or /ai-hub/ (topic-matched)
- /guest/* → /cloud-labs/
- /exam-qa/old-slugs → /exam-qa/
- /full-courses/old-slugs → matching /certifications/ page
- /ai-hub/v/* → /ai-hub/ (old video sub-pages)
- /ai-hub/old-categories → /ai-hub/ or /prompt-guide/
- /courses, /full-courses → /certifications/

Uses wildcard routes where possible to catch future variants.

chat: fix body tag leaking into rendered markdown when content ends with code block (#309336)

* chat: fix body tag leaking into rendered markdown when content ends with code block

When markdown content ends with a fenced code block, the closing backticks
were immediately followed by </body> on the same line, so CommonMark didn't
recognize the fence as closed. Add \n\n before </body> to ensure proper
parsing. Add regression test.

commit.message:
Fix HTML.KpiCard SYNTAXERROR: remove @keyframes from DAX body

The TMDL parser treats '@' as a special reserved character for verbatim
strings. The '@keyframes' CSS rule embedded in the DAX string caused the
TMDL parser to throw a SYNTAXERROR even though it was inside a quoted
string literal. Removed the @keyframes animation block and the 'animation'
property from .status-dot, and cleaned up the now-unused _pulseColor and
_pulseTransparent DAX variables.

5. Monitors health with live dashboard

7 scenarios:
  1. Bad deploy crash (SCADA bug) → release trigger
  2. Bad deploy perf (checksum loop) → release trigger
  3. Bad deploy config (wrong port) → release trigger
  4. Disk pressure (VM) → Azure Monitor alert
  5. Organic load spike (no bug) → Azure Monitor alert

commit.message:
Implementar base de datos Room para caché persistente offline del mapa y la red de calles (#13)

* Add RoadNetworkCache and VM integration

Introduce a persistent RoadNetworkCache (disk-based, TTL, LRU per ~2km cells) and integrate it into WorldMapViewModel via a Context-aware Factory. Configure osmdroid caching and parameters in MainActivity and use the ViewModel.Factory when creating the view model. Improve OverpassRepository: expand fetch radius to 2000m, increase read timeout, better HTTP error handling, URLEncoding, and parsing optimizations. Update WorldMapViewModel to prefer cache on startup, save downloads to cache, add refetch cooldown/thresholds, and restructure the game loop. UI refinements in WorldMapScreen: use the Factory for the ViewModel, marker caching, smoother zoom animations, WebView JS calls formatting, layout/style polish, and loading/zoom behavior. Also add zoom constants in WorldMapState.

* Migrate road cache to Room; add tile cache

Replace the old file/JSON road-network cache with a Room-backed implementation and add a new per-provider tile cache.

- RoadNetworkCache: switched from file-based JSON to Room (uses RoadZone/RoadWay/RoadNode entities and RoadNetworkDao). Implements TTL checks, LRU eviction via DAO, atomic insertZoneWithData transaction, and reconstitution of MapWay from DB rows.
- Added PowDatabase (Room) created under filesDir so DB is persistent and excluded from external cleaners.
- Added TileCache: SQLite-backed tile storage per provider with TTL and simple LRU eviction, stored under filesDir as provider.mbtiles.
- Added Room entities (RoadEntities.kt) and DAO (RoadNetworkDao.kt) to support the new cache.
- MainMenuScreen: UI overhaul (layout tweaks, settings panel, provider selector) and now passes chosen MapProvider when navigating to the map.
- MainActivity: OSMDroid configuration simplified (uses app filesDir, explicit cache params), navigation transitions cleaned, and world map provider is applied to WorldMapViewModel; minor permission/location call formatting cleaned up.
- build.gradle.kts: added ksp plugin and Room dependencies (runtime/ktx + ksp compiler), reorganized some dependencies and simplified compileSdk setting.

These changes centralize persistent map data in Room/SQLite for reliability and atomicity, add a robust tile cache, and let users choose map providers via the main menu.

* Fix review issues: composite PK, TileCache context, SHA-256 URL key, CacheStatusWidget, DAO location, .gitignore

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/gabrielhuav/PolitecnicoOpenWorld/sessions/89d7cab6-4830-4f6b-a113-948e9ad1a9c4

Co-authored-by: gabrielhuav <71236850+gabrielhuav@users.noreply.github.com>

* Use application context in TileCache

Store the application context (appContext) in TileCache instead of a generic Context to avoid leaking activity contexts. Updated tilesDir initialization and the TileDatabase SQLiteOpenHelper to use appContext/applicationContext and made the constructor parameter private.

* Migrate tile cache to Room (MapTileEntity/DAO)

Replace the old file/SQLite-based TileCache with a Room-backed implementation: add MapTileEntity and MapTileDao, refactor TileCache to use MapTileDao (insert/get/count/deleteOldest), and remove the custom SQLiteOpenHelper logic. Improve CachingWebViewClient to normalize tile URLs, hash the normalized URL for cache keys, only intercept GET requests, refine MIME guessing and logging, and download using the original URL. Wire the new DAO into PowDatabase (add mapTileDao, include MapTileEntity) and bump DB version to 3; update WorldMapViewModel to obtain the mapTileDao and pass it into TileCache. Includes LRU eviction via DAO when MAX_TILES_PER_PROVIDER is exceeded.

* Add cache & FPS widget toggles and sync UI

Introduce showCacheWidget and showFpsWidget state and controls across the main menu and world map. MainMenuState and WorldMapState gain new flags, MainMenuViewModel exposes toggleCacheWidget/toggleFpsWidget and MainMenu UI now lets users enable/disable cache and FPS widgets before starting. MainActivity navigation callback updated to pass cache/fps choices to WorldMapViewModel, and WorldMapViewModel adds update/toggle functions to apply them. WorldMapScreen now computes real-time FPS (when enabled) and shows/hides diagnostic widgets (cache status and FPS) with animated visibility; also contains small UI/formatting cleanups. Function signatures and some composables were adjusted to wire the new options end-to-end.

* Fix composite PK for tiles, empty cache guard, encoding fix, remove redundant withContext

commit.message:
Fix WGPU SIGSEGV, add layer-by-layer validation, update quality comparison

- src/bin/pipeline.rs, src/bin/infer.rs: Use POSIX _exit(0) to bypass NVIDIA
  Vulkan atexit handler that causes SIGSEGV on process teardown (exit code 139).
  Fixes false FAIL in quality comparison script for all WGPU backends.

- src/model/dit.rs: Add BlockDebugOutputs<B>, forward_with_cond_debug() for
  per-DiT-block output capture during validation.

- src/bin/validate.rs: Add per-block checks (block_0_out, block_1_out),
  after_in_proj check, and KV-cache consistency test (cached vs uncached).
  All 10 checks pass with max_abs_diff < 1e-3.

- scripts/validate_numerics.py: Register forward hooks to capture per-block
  outputs and in_proj intermediates; export 13 tensors to validate_tensors.safetensors.

- scripts/run_quality_comparison.py: Add Python bf16 backend support; add
  --skip-python and --force flags; upgrade to 25/25 success reporting.

- docs/feature-coverage.md: Update multi-backend comparison table with new
  timing data (all 25/25 successful). Document Python bf16 failure (cuBLAS
  CUDA_R_16BF not supported on RTX A6000), WGPU SIGSEGV fix, and CUDA JIT overhead.

- docs/analysis/layer-comparison.md: New — documents layer-by-layer methodology,
  all-PASS results, and RNG explanation for audio variation between backends.

- justfile: Update validate recipe comment.
